title: Geographic Workforce Performance Comparison
dataSources: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream вАФ Metrics (Endpoint tests)
spl:
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.domain="endpoint"
| stats avg(network.latency) as avg_latency_s avg(network.loss) as avg_loss avg(network.score) as avg_score count as agent_count by thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.country.iso_code, thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.region.iso_code
| eval avg_latency_ms=round(avg_latency_s*1000,1)
| sort avg_score
description: Comparing digital experience metrics across office locations and regions identifies sites with persistent network quality issues, enabling targeted infrastructure improvements.
value: Comparing digital experience metrics across office locations and regions identifies sites with persistent network quality issues, enabling targeted infrastructure improvements.
implementation: Endpoint agent metrics include geographic attributes: `thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.country.iso_code` and `thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.region.iso_code`. Aggregate network quality metrics by region to identify poorly performing locations. Combine with `thousandeyes.source.agent.location` for more specific site-level analysis.
visualization: Map (score by region), Table (region, score, latency, loss, agent count), Column chart comparing regions.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
вАҐ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719).
вАҐ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream вАФ Metrics (Endpoint tests).
вАҐ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 вАФ Configure data collection
Endpoint agent metrics include geographic attributes: `thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.country.iso_code` and `thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.region.iso_code`. Aggregate network quality metrics by region to identify poorly performing locations. Combine with `thousandeyes.source.agent.location` for more specific site-level analysis.

Step 2 вАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.domain="endpoint"
| stats avg(network.latency) as avg_latency_s avg(network.loss) as avg_loss avg(network.score) as avg_score count as agent_count by thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.country.iso_code, thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.region.iso_code
| eval avg_latency_ms=round(avg_latency_s*1000,1)
| sort avg_score
```

Understanding this SPL

**Geographic Workforce Performance Comparison** вАФ Comparing digital experience metrics across office locations and regions identifies sites with persistent network quality issues, enabling targeted infrastructure improvements.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream вАФ Metrics (Endpoint tests). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedвАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

вАҐ Invokes macro `stream_index` вАФ in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
вАҐ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.country.iso_code, thousandeyes.source.agent.geo.region.iso_code** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
вАҐ `eval` defines or adjusts **avg_latency_ms** вАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
вАҐ Orders rows with `sort` вАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 вАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 вАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Map (score by region), Table (region, score, latency, loss, agent count), Column chart comparing regions.
grandmaExplanation: Comparing digital experience metrics across office locations and regions identifies sites with persistent network quality issues, enabling targeted infrastructure improvements вАФ so outages, slowdowns, and risky changes get flagged in time to act.
